description: A real outdoor photograph of the Frances Perkins Department of Labor Building in Washington, D.C., shot from below against a bright blue sky with scattered white clouds. The mid-century concrete government building spans the frame, its name 'FRANCES PERKINS DEPARTMENT OF LABOR BUILDING' carved into the stone base. A large American flag hangs vertically at center between flagpoles. Flanking it are two enormous maroon vertical banners installed on the facade: the left banner shows a stylized portrait of Donald Trump with text 'AMERICA 250' and 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST'; the right banner shows a matching portrait of Theodore Roosevelt with the same 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST' / 'America 250' branding. A lone figure stands at the base. While the building displays designed propaganda banners, the image itself is a candid photograph of the building rather than a standalone designed graphic; the on-image text comes from the physical banners. DOL posted it as a Labor Day greeting, associating the agency and the holiday with the administration's 'America 250' / 'American Workers First' messaging for a general patriotic audience.
notable_text: AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST | AMERICA 250 | FRANCES PERKINS DEPARTMENT OF LABOR BUILDING
